title: tRPC: receipt.create / get / confirm + async parse
description: receipt.create: persist Receipt(PROCESSING), fire-and-forget parse (call broccoli-model with image URL + itemTypes), return receiptId. receipt.get: poll status + items. receipt.confirm: save user-corrected items, set SAVED. Includes price '$12.98'->float parsing and category->ItemType mapping.

title: Make broccoli-model stateless (accept itemTypes in /ocr)
description: Refactor broccoli-model /ocr to accept the ItemType catalog in the request payload instead of reading Postgres, and stop writing the OCR-text Receipt row. Honors 'broccoli-api is the single writer'. api passes the catalog it owns. Keep x-api-key auth.
close_reason: Model-side refactor complete & merged in broccoli-model#11: /ocr is stateless (accepts itemTypes:[{name,category}] in payload, no Receipt write, no core-DB read, x-api-key kept). API-side catalog-passing when calling /ocr is tracked under 2r8.5.

comments.author: Afrothundr
comments.text: Model-side refactor done: broccoli-model /ocr now stateless — accepts itemTypes:[{name,category}] in payload, no Receipt write, no core-DB read, x-api-key kept. PR: Afrothundr/broccoli-model#11. Remaining for this task = none on model side; API passes the catalog when the /ocr call is wired in 2r8.5.

title: Wire UploadThing for receipt images
description: Server-side UploadThing so the app can upload a receipt photo and the api gets a fetchable URL + key. Store url/key on Receipt; key enables later deletion. broccoli-model fetches the URL.
close_reason: Wired UploadThing (v7 fetch adapter). New src/uploadthing.ts defines the receiptImage FileRouter (image, 8MB, 1 file) with a better-auth-gated middleware (rejects unauthenticated) and onUploadComplete returning {uploadedBy,url,key}. Mounted on Hono at ALL /api/uploadthing; OurFileRouter type exported via package './uploadthing' for the mobile client. UPLOADTHING_TOKEN documented in .env.example + README. Receipt persistence with url/key is deferred to receipt.create (2r8.5). Verified: typecheck clean; server boots; GET /api/uploadthing returns the receiptImage route config.

title: Seed ItemType from USDA FoodKeeper (CC0)
description: ETL the FoodKeeper JSON (data.gov, CC0) into ItemType: map name/category, collapse location-aware ranges to a single representative lifespan for now, set source='FoodKeeper', stash the raw FoodKeeper record in raw Json. Idempotent upsert seed script. ~400 items. Note: clinical names make the parser pick-list noisier — track as a tuning follow-up.
close_reason: Seeded 661 ItemTypes from USDA FoodKeeper (CC0). Vendored faithful source JSON (prisma/seeds/foodkeeper.json); seed.ts derives name (subtitle-disambiguated, all unique), category (14 top-level), representative lifespan (collapsed from FoodKeeper timeframes; Indefinitely->10y; 10 durationless items left 0 for Phase 3 LLM fallback), storageAdvice, source, raw. Idempotent (deleteMany+createMany). Verified against ephemeral Postgres.

title: Prisma models + migration: Receipt, Item, ItemType
description: Add Receipt (userId, imageUrl, imageKey, status enum PROCESSING/READY/SAVED/ERROR, storeName, purchasedAt, total, rawExtraction Json), Item (receiptId, userId, name, price, quantity, unit, category), and ItemType (name unique, category, suggestedLifeSpanSeconds, storageAdvice, source, raw Json). Location-agnostic for now; raw holds the FoodKeeper record for Phase 3 expansion. Generate migration.
close_reason: Added Receipt, Item, ItemType models + ReceiptStatus enum; migration 20260630172917_add_receipt_item_itemtype verified against ephemeral Postgres (tables, FKs, indexes present); typecheck passes.

title: Phase 1 — Capture → extract → review (broccoli-api)
description: API side of the core loop: receive a receipt image, run it through broccoli-model, persist extracted items, and let the app save a corrected list. Decisions locked: images on UploadThing; parsing async + poll; broccoli-model made stateless (api passes ItemType catalog); ItemType seeded from USDA FoodKeeper (CC0), location-agnostic schema for now (single lifespan + storageAdvice + source + raw JSON), expand to pantry/fridge/freezer at Phase 3. Done when: a real receipt becomes saved, corrected Items in Postgres via broccoli-api.

title: Deploy broccoli-api to Railway (start + migrate-on-release)
description: Railway service: start command 'npm start', release runs 'npm run db:deploy'. Confirm public URL serves /health.
notes: Service broccoli-api created (repo-linked, domain https://broccoli-api-production.up.railway.app, all env vars set, railway.json migrate-on-release). BLOCKED: no GitHub webhook installed — auto-deploy needs the Railway GitHub App authorized in the dashboard (browser). Alt: one-time 'railway up' to verify build now.
close_reason: Deployed & verified on Railway. Root cause of first failure: Railway used Node 18, incompatible with tsx --env-file-if-exists; pinned engines.node>=22. Live at https://broccoli-api-production.up.railway.app — /health, /trpc/health, /api/auth/* all 200; internal.ping correctly UNAUTHORIZED without token. GitHub auto-deploy confirmed.

title: Run initial Prisma migration against Railway Postgres
description: npm run db:migrate locally to create migration, then db:deploy on Railway. broccoli-api owns all migrations.
close_reason: Done: 0_init migration (better-auth tables) generated via migrate diff + applied via migrate deploy to broccoli_api. Verified 5 tables present.

title: Provision Railway Postgres + Redis; set env vars
description: Create Railway project services for Postgres and Redis. Set DATABASE_URL, BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_URL, INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN. Needs interactive 'railway login'.
close_reason: Done: fresh broccoli_api DB created in existing core-db instance (template0, to dodge collation mismatch); Redis already present; DATABASE_URL (private), BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_URL, INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN set on the broccoli-api service.

title: Scaffold service: Hono + tRPC + Prisma + better-auth
description: Node/TS scaffold with /health, /api/auth/*, tRPC router (health/me/internal.ping), token-gated internalProcedure for scheduler calls.

title: Phase 0 — Foundations (broccoli-api)
description: Stand up broccoli-api: scaffold, Railway deploy with fresh Postgres, better-auth, and a health/auth surface the mobile app can reach. Done when the Expo app can authenticate and hit /health.
close_reason: Phase 0 complete: broccoli-api scaffolded, deployed to Railway with Postgres, better-auth for mobile, /health + auth verified reachable from a device.

title: Verify core loop API-side end to end
description: With a real receipt image URL, exercise create->parse->get(READY)->confirm and confirm Items persist correctly in Postgres. API side of Phase 1 'done when'.

title: Verify /health + auth reachable from a device
description: From broccoli-mobile dev build, hit /health and complete a sign-in against the deployed broccoli-api. This is the api side of Phase 0 'done when'.
close_reason: Verified from Android dev build: /health reachable and sign-in completes against deployed broccoli-api (Railway).

title: Configure better-auth for mobile sessions
description: Verify email/password sign-up + session works; tune session expiry for long-lived mobile sessions; set trustedOrigins for the app.
close_reason: Registered server-side @better-auth/expo expo() plugin (pairs with mobile expoClient), tuned sessions to 30d expiry/1d updateAge, kept broccolimobile:// trustedOrigins. Verified end-to-end against ephemeral Postgres: sign-up/email creates user, session cookie authorizes tRPC me, expiresAt confirms 30d window, no-cookie returns UNAUTHORIZED.
